title: Tabernaemontana hystrix
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana hystrix is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Tabernaemontana, which consists of approximately 50 species. The species T. hystrix is native to tropical regions in Asia and Australia. It is a member of the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. The classification of T. hystrix is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies.
uses: Tabernaemontana hystrix has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is said to have antimalarial and antibacterial properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In some regions, Tabernaemontana hystrix is also used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant's roots and leaves are often used in traditional medicine, and it is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications and benefits.

intro: Tabernaemontana hystrix, also known as the spiny tree tabernaemontana or tree of life, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its potential to treat fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana hystrix include alkaloids, such as voacangine and voacristine, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tabernaemontana hystrix is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the form of a powder or capsule, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
